termlists._comment: NO: 'språk' is added here as in Norwegian a lot of the same terms are used for language conservation as nature conservation (e.g. forvaltningsområde). This also concerns 'protection', hence the addition of 'barnevernet' (NO: child protective services).

NO: 'språk' is added here as in Norwegian a lot of the same terms are used for language conservation as nature conservation (e.g. forvaltningsområde). This also concerns 'protection', hence the addition of 'barnevernet' (NO: child protective services).
'forest' is changed for rain forest, boreal etc. to help avoid random forest, kelp forest

EN: 'area' is combined, cut away 95 % noisy results. NO: Added 'natur' and 'kulturlandskap'(NO) instead of 'område'(NO) because there were too many results from cultural/human conservation. 'landskap'(NO) alone got a lot of cultural results, so it is combined to specific types.

Fishing terms are limited to freshwater. NO: Salmon and trout included as partially freshwater and important species there.
'ved' (NO: firewood) is difficult in Norwegian because it is often combined with other words (can't stop truncation), and it is a preposition.

targets._comment: Includes an additional term list (termlist15_8d) specific for using these strings for Norwegian research. It contains Latin and Norwegian names for terrestrial and limnic species deemed 'very high risk' in the Norwegian black list of introduced species (2018, newest version; https://www.artsdatabanken.no/fremmedartslista2018). Note that this may result in bias if used for other countries. NO: A NINA report was used to help with Norwegian terminology: Blaalid, R., Often, A., Magnussen, K., Olsen, S. L. & Westergaard, K. B. 2017. Fremmede skadelige karplanter - Bekjempelsesmetodikk og spredningshindrende tiltak. NINA Rapport 1432. 87 s. http://hdl.handle.net/11250/2469573
